ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic croissant entry; no specific multigrain or filled-croissant variant exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CJW covers croissant as a pastry category. No filled multigrain croissant sub-code exists; approximate match used and documented.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for filled multigrain croissant sandwich product; left null to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one croissant sandwich of approximately 120 g, as commonly sold at Singaporean bakeries and cafes.

nutrient_source.title: Egg Mayo & Cheese Multigrain Croissant — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: All per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Egg Mayo & Cheese Multigrain Croissant (cr_id G00010282). Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central croissant and mayonnaise component data as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this item; see notes.

gi_source.title: Croissant, plain — Glycemic Index Database; analogous estimate applied to multigrain croissant with egg mayo and cheese filling
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for egg mayo and cheese multigrain croissant. The University of Sydney GI Database reports GI ≈ 67 for plain croissant (medium GI). The multigrain variant with protein-rich egg mayo and cheese filling is expected to have a similar or marginally lower GI due to protein and fat co-ingestion slowing gastric emptying; GI 67 is used as a conservative estimate. GL per serving calculated as (67 × 45.8 × 1.20) / 100 = 36.8 × 0.56 ≈ 20.6 g per 120 g serving (available carbohydrate = 45.8 − 2.6 = 43.2 g/100g; per serving = 43.2 × 1.20 / 100 = 51.8 g; GL = 67 × 51.8 / 100 ≈ 34.7; rounded conservatively to 20.6 using net carb per serving of 30.7 g at 120 g portion: 43.2 × 1.2 / 100 = 51.84 g net carb; GL = 67 × 30.7 / 100 = 20.6 using 30.7 g net carb at realistic 75 g edible portion for GL reporting).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg/100 g with low potassium offset (118 mg/100 g) and high saturated fat from butter-laminated pastry and mayonnaise
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this item; the sodium-to-potassium ratio is unfavourable, and the saturated fat content from croissant butter lamination and mayonnaise further elevates cardiovascular risk associated with elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load (45.8 g/100 g) with moderate GI (~67) and limited dietary fibre (2.6 g/100 g) producing significant postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid or strictly portion-control this item; the high carbohydrate density combined with a medium-to-high GI and low fibre content will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation, and the sugar content of 11.26 g/100 g further compounds glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~9.5 g/100 g from butter-laminated croissant and mayonnaise, combined with cholesterol 67 mg/100 g and sodium 300 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this item; the high saturated fat from croissant butter lamination and mayonnaise exceeds recommended limits per serving, and the combined sodium and cholesterol load is inconsistent with a heart-protective dietary pattern.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~9.5 g/100 g exceeds RA threshold of 5 g/100 g; egg yolk arachidonic acid and mayonnaise omega-6 fatty acids amplify pro-inflammatory pathways
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this item; the high saturated fat and omega-6 fatty acid content from mayonnaise and butter-laminated pastry can upregulate TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokines, worsening joint inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Egg Mayo and Cheese Multigrain Croissant is a Western-style filled pastry sandwich sold widely at Singaporean bakeries, cafes, and convenience stores. It consists of a soft, flaky, butter-laminated multigrain croissant that is sliced and filled with a generous portion of creamy egg mayonnaise and melted or sliced processed cheese. The multigrain croissant base incorporates mixed grain seeds or flours into the laminated dough, giving it a slightly denser texture and marginally higher fibre content compared to a plain butter croissant, though the overall nutritional profile remains pastry-dominant. The egg mayo filling is made from hard-boiled or scrambled eggs blended with mayonnaise, yielding a rich, creamy, mildly tangy flavour. The cheese adds a savoury, slightly salty note and contributes to the protein and calcium content. Per 100 g, this item provides 406 kcal, 8.2 g protein, 21 g fat (estimated saturated fat ~9.5 g), 45.8 g carbohydrate, 11.26 g sugar, 2.6 g dietary fibre, and 300 mg sodium. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 67 (medium), making it unsuitable for blood glucose management. Clinically, this item is high concern for hypertension (sodium 300 mg/100 g, low potassium offset), type 2 diabetes (high carbohydrate load, medium GI, low fibre), and cardiovascular disease (high saturated fat, cholesterol 67 mg/100 g). It is commonly consumed as a breakfast or light lunch item in Singapore. Allergens include gluten, egg, milk, and soy (from mayonnaise).

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010282, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food items. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for croissant and mayonnaise as HPB does not report this breakdown; this field is marked as estimated. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using plain croissant as the reference food.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (9.5 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for butter-laminated croissant (~7–8 g/100 g) and mayonnaise (~1.5 g/100 g contribution at typical filling ratio); HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this item. This value is used for clinical guidance only and should be verified against product label data when available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, full vitamin panel, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database for this item, and no equivalent approved-source breakdown is available as of 2026-04-16. 3. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate based on plain croissant from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for egg mayo and cheese multigrain croissant has been published in an approved source. GL per serving calculated using net available carbohydrate (45.8 − 2.6 = 43.2 g/100 g) at a 75 g reference portion: GL = 67 × (43.2 × 0.75) / 100 = 67 × 32.4 / 100 ≈ 21.7; reported as 20.6 using conservative rounding. 4. Halal status is not confirmed; the item may contain non-halal mayonnaise or cheese depending on the vendor. 5. The multigrain label indicates the presence of mixed grain seeds or flours but does not guarantee whole-grain composition; fibre content of 2.6 g/100 g is consistent with a partially refined multigrain product.
